items.title: Rural suburb flagged as home for proposed $40b 'hyperscale' data centre

children.content: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

gallery.altText: An AI generated image of the outside of the proposed facility.

thumbnail.altText: An AI generated image of the outside of the proposed facility.

avatar.alt: A journalist standing inside a television news studio, she is wearing a white blazer and smiling at the camera.

items.title: NT to become last jurisdiction to legalise voluntary assisted dying

children.content: The Northern Territory is set to make voluntary assisted dying legal in August. The ABC understands all 17 Country Liberal Party government members will vote in favour of the Rights of the Terminally Ill Act.

image.altText: VAD brochure

items.title: Woolworths charged over NT crash between forklift, motorcyclist

children.content: Woolworths has been hit with a string of workplace safety charges after one of the supermarket giant's forklift operators allegedly struck a motorcyclist on a public road, causing "serious injuries", in 2025.

image.altText: Emergency services surround a crashed red motorcycle on a public street.

items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby child protection review finds departmental failings

children.content: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, an interim report into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman wearing a police uniform

articles.title: Protest held ahead of NT child protection laws debate

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is debating controversial child protection laws that have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: A large crowd of protesters standing on a grassed area, holding signs.

gallery.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

thumbnail.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

items.title: Police confirm investigation involving incident with NT administrator

children.content: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and jacket behind a microphone

items.title: PM backs bid for future NT AFL team, no promises on funding

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

image.altText: The Tiwi Islands footy team with Anthony Albanese.

items.title: Sixty bikes stolen from Darwin community organisation

children.content: About 60 donated bicycles have been stolen from the Darwin Men's Shed storage site, delaying the organisation's plans to support remote communities.

gallery.altText: A man in a blue shirt in a workshop

thumbnail.altText: A man in a blue shirt in a workshop

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Critical breeding breakthrough welcome news for lovers of purple passionfruit

children.content: An unpalatable future has been averted for Australians who love purple-skinned passionfruit, with new research ensuring better prospects for the delicious fruit.

image.altText: A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

articles.title: How one of Iran's 'Axis' allies is threatening to widen the war

children.content: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

image.altText: A satellite image showing a narrow strait of water between two continents.

articles.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

items.title: Hanson labels domestic violence 'two-way street'

children.content: The One Nation leader says women should not "put up with" domestic violence, as she highlights the cost-of-living pressures on families.

image.altText: A man and a woman at a press conference

items.title: Details of allegations against Alan Jones emerge after judge decision

children.content: Details of allegations against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones can be revealed, after a non-publication order was lifted by a judge.

image.altText: An old man in a suit walks with his male lawyer amongst a media scrum.

items.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE diagnosis

children.content: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

image.altText: Paul Wade

items.title: Grass 'skeletons' hint at 25,000-year-old Indigenous fire ritual in cave

children.content: Evidence of ancient burnt grasses unearthed in a Victorian cave suggest the old ancestors of the GunaiKurnai people used the cave for fire rituals during the Ice Age.

image.altText: GunaiKurnai Elder Uncle Russell Mullett at the cave entrance of Cloggs Cave

items.title: As The Odyssey smashes box office records, one name is drawing people in

children.content: The Odyssey's director Sir Christopher Nolan has a stellar catalogue. Interstellar, even. But as his new film smashes box office records, is it safe to crown him the new king of Hollywood?

gallery.altText: Christopher Nolan on set with arm up, Matt Damon and Zendaya in character off to one side

items.title: 'Why would I do that?': Boy in court charged with murdering mother, stabbing sister

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

image.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway

items.title: Private 'space port' to test guided weapons in Queensland outback

children.content: Black Sky Aerospace will construct a new airstrip and explosives storage to support defence and aerospace testing within the state.

items.title: Lewis breaks Australian 100m record, beats five-time Olympic champ

children.content: Torrie Lewis shows she is hitting her straps ahead of the Commonwealth Games by beating her own record mark during a meet in Slovakia.

articles.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

children.content: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

image.altText: Cameron McEvoy at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacs swimming trials.

articles.title: Fox sisters combine in 'dream team' for gold medals at World Championships

articles.title: Spanish chief accuses FIFA boss of 'destroying the football industry'

children.content: Women who have lost children talk about continuing in the wake of such grief, and how people stepped up, or stepped away.

items.title: Judge says there's a 'real risk' to safety of Bondi Beach attack witnesses

children.content: Twenty witnesses in the Bondi Beach terror attack case have been granted anonymity, with a judge saying there is a "real risk" to their safety.

image.altText: Flowers laid in tribute to bondi victims.

children.content: When construction finishes next year, the Sydney building will be the tallest hybrid structure in the world by 100 metres.

items.title: Network 10 moves 10 News+ from weekdays

children.content: Network 10 says its 10 News+ news and current affairs program is moving from its nightly weekday slot and will be aired on Sunday nights only.

items.title: 'These things didn't happen': Senior Catholic cleric denies sex abuse

children.content: The lawyer for a former Catholic bishop who allegedly sexually abused three young men tells his trial the incidents did not happen, and warns the jury not to be affected by possible prejudice against the church.

image.altText: A mid-shot of former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Saunders, outside court wearing a dark coat, grey scarf and grey flat cap.

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

items.title: Doctor reveals injuries of woman allegedly sexually assaulted by Yung Filly

children.content: Graphic photographs of injuries allegedly inflicted by an internationally renowned YouTube star on an infatuated fan in his hotel room after a concert are shown to a jury at his sexual assault trial in a Perth court.

image.altText: A man with African appearence leaves a court building.

items.title: $16 million mansion set for demolition catches fire twice in a night

children.content: Fire broke out twice in one night at a $16-million mansion on the NSW Mid North Coast. It was approved for demolition just weeks ago, and police are treating the blazes as suspicious.

image.altText: A white house on the top of a hill with smoke lit up orange by a fire from another nearby house behind it at night.

items.title: Drink spiking charge against Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun thrown out

children.content: A Victorian magistrate has struck out a drink spiking charge against Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun.

image.altText: A woman wearing an outlandish hat

items.title: Jail for acccountant who gambled away $878,000 stolen from IGA

image.altText: Ms Labuts with her solicitor outside Newcastle District Court.

items.title: Government reveals proposed design for new aquatics centre for 2032 Games

items.title: Twelve dolphins now found dead in southern WA

children.content: Tasmania's health department is challenging a coronial review into allegations that a clinical director falsified death certificates at Launceston General Hospital.

image.altText: Dr Peter Renshaw sits in front of a microphone.

items.title: Six beluga whales make successful journey by air from Canada to US

children.content: Six beluga whales from a defunct marine park in Canada have been successfully moved to new homes in the US.
